description: Write an Episode page for the Portland trip
subject: Portland trip

checkpoints.description:
Snapshot the photo directory and location history. Create enriched source pages — catalog photos (count, date range, EXIF quality), assess geolocation coverage and accuracy. Note platform limitations and data quality issues.

Plan your editorial approach for the episode page.

Write the episode page for the Portland trip using photos and location history. Build a day-by-day itinerary from timestamps and GPS data. Embed key photos in the sections they relate to.

On the Talk page, note gaps where additional sources (messages, transactions) would fill in context.

Snapshot messages and transactions. Create enriched source pages for each.

Integrate the new data into the existing episode page — weave social context from messages and spending patterns from transactions into the existing itinerary. Do not append separate sections for each source; place facts where they belong in the narrative.

Cross-reference across sources (e.g., a restaurant transaction + a photo at the same location + a message about dinner plans). Update the Talk page with resolved gaps and new findings.

Create person stub pages for key trip participants identified in messages and photos. Each stub should have an infobox with known details and a brief description of their role in the trip.

Link person stubs from the episode page. Update the episode page's People section.

The wiki owner has provided personal memories about the Portland trip. Integrate this testimony into the episode page.

Cite owner statements using {{Cite testimony|speaker=...|date=...}}. Where the owner's account conflicts with digital evidence (e.g., a different restaurant name than what the transaction shows), note the discrepancy on the Talk page.

Update the narrative with personal context that digital sources alone couldn't capture.

Final editorial review. Audit the episode page for timeline consistency, tone, and completeness. Ensure all factual claims have citations.

Produce a citation manifest pairing every factual claim with its source evidence. Write the manifest to `./citation-manifest.json`.
